body_txt: Emotions help us relate to other people, and in fiction, they help us relate to and understand the characters. Some characters are Hot-Blooded , or they might be melancholic, or perhaps a seething cauldron of repressed rage, just waiting to boil over. We may not always agree with these characters, but on an emotional level, we at least comprehend how they feel and what motivates them. There are some characters, like The Stoic or The Spock , who either suppress emotion or even claim not to have them. One would assume that these characters would be difficult to relate to, but they tend to be very popular with fans. Then you have the ever-calm character. They're not The Stoic or The Spock , concealing their emotions. This person's feelings exist and can be understood; they simply keep their calm and talk to people in a steady, often reassuring voice. Indeed, their voices are actually considered warm and friendly (though a charismatic Villain with Good Publicity can be very effective at this type of speech pattern as well). Just as the Implacable Man can shrug off injuries and attacks that would cripple most people, this person can shrug off stress and anxiety that would have most of us an emotional wreck. This is not often a primary protagonist, though it could be a member of their peer group. Audiences prefer heroes to whom they can relate, and while many a hero can think on their feet and stay calm in a crisis, it serves the writers to have their emotions be more on display for the benefit of the audience. They may engage in Casual Danger Dialogue , and is perfectly capable of asking their opponent about their day as though they were a Friendly Enemy , even if that's not the case. They'll likely be the Blue Oni in a Red Oni, Blue Oni pair, or the soft-spoken in a Energetic and Soft-Spoken Duo . In some works, they're likely to overlap with Erudite Stoner . Of course, someone who can maintain their calm, even in their speech, in a crisis is someone who can often think clearly at the same time. And they can easily be a case of Beware the Nice Ones . Villainous versions are often either a Manipulative Bastard or a Smug Snake . Similar to Mellow Fellow , but while the Mellow Fellow is laid-back and relaxed at all times, the Ever-Calm can show that they are upset and even frustrated, but tend to handle it in a very thoughtful, calm manner. The ever-calm is just very skilled at managing stress so as not to let it overwhelm them, while not necessarily being laid-back about a situation. The writer can of course have the character they've portrayed previously as ever-calm suddenly lose their cool to demonstrate that OOC Is Serious Business , similar to the use of Not So Stoic . Compare Cozy Voice for Catastrophes and Soft-Spoken Sadist . Compare and contrast Dissonant Serenity if the character's calmness is unsettling rather than reassuring. Contrast Suddenly Shouting , Baritone of Strength , and Authority Sounds Deep . See also Nerves of Steel and Only Sane Man .
